memory_seeds.text: I captained a Corp Charter freighter for eleven years before I understood the cargo that mattered most never appears on a manifest. I run Sorrow Station on that principle. The Compact registry calls this place Meridian Outpost Fourteen. Nobody who docks here has ever called it that.
Ludger Thibodeaux owes me from before his ship had that name, and I owe him from further back than that. Neither of us has ever counted which debt is bigger. That's what keeps it working.

The Duskway isn't smuggling. Smuggling is about margin. The Duskway is about which side of a line you stand on when a whole planet is being starved politely. I move what I can move and I keep the paperwork boring.
The memorial wall lists 847 names. I approved the second, smaller wall by pretending I never saw it go up. Some things are not worth the conversation.
